arts.ttl: Former Carlisle United man announces retirement
arts.sum: Former Carlisle United man Kelvin Langmead, who scored in one of the most dramatic games of the 2003/04 season, has announced his retirement.

arts.ttl: Kennedy, the ginger Lampard, made his mark on Carlisle United
arts.sum: JON COLMAN: There may have been more stylish players, but the goalscoring midfielder, who has retired, brought real substance.

arts.ttl: Former Carlisle United keeper Holy joins new club
arts.sum: Former Carlisle United goalkeeper Tomas Holy has found a new club in his native Czech Republic.

arts.ttl: Barrow chairman responds to Carlisle United sporting director rumour
arts.sum: Barrow chairman Paul Hornby has responded to reports linking sporting director Iain Wood with Carlisle United.

arts.ttl: Colchester boss Cowley has his say on Williamson and Carlisle United
arts.sum: Colchester United boss Danny Cowley expects Mike Williamson to lead Carlisle United to the top end of League Two despite the Blues’ poor start.

arts.ttl: Latest on midfield duo's fitness bid at Carlisle United
arts.sum: Ethan Robson and Taylor Charters are being “drip-fed” back into training as they target their Carlisle United comebacks.

arts.ttl: Carlisle United fan tackles 5k-a-day challenge for cancer charity
arts.sum: Carlisle United supporter Reece Oxland is running 5km every day this month to raise money for Prostate Cancer UK.

arts.ttl: 'Already convinced' - Williamson on Carlisle United's prospects
arts.sum: Mike Williamson says he's “already convinced” that Carlisle United’s players can make the improvements needed despite recent results.

arts.ttl: In pictures: Carlisle United fans at the Notts County game!
arts.sum: A crowd of 5,594 watched Carlisle United's latest home game as they went down to a 2-0 defeat to Notts County.

arts.ttl: AFC Wimbledon say Carlisle United game set to be ON after flood repairs
arts.sum: Carlisle United's game at AFC Wimbledon looks set to be ON as the London club continue repairs to their flood-hit pitch.
